summary: The sole persistent SAST finding is Flask debug mode (codeql:py/flask-debug), which is an off-target CWE-215 finding not in the target CWE list (CWE-862, CWE-89, CWE-94, CWE-352, CWE-502). Across 3 attempts (problem_solver + 2 security_fixer), the code remained unchanged at line 121 (app.run(debug=True)), indicating a fixer loop where the security_fixer role failed to address or was unable to resolve this finding. The SAST tool correctly flags the debug mode as a security risk, but the scenario's primary attack surface (storage/filesystem) and target CWEs are not impacted by this finding.
suggested_actions: Change app.run(debug=True) to app.run(debug=False) or remove the debug=True argument entirely to resolve the codeql:py/flask-debug finding.
If the security_fixer role is ignoring or unable to fix this finding, update the fixer prompt to explicitly require addressing all SAST findings, including off-target ones.
Consider adding a comment or configuration to suppress the finding if debug mode is intentionally required for non-production use, but ensure it is not present in production code.

evidence.description: A Flask app appears to be run in debug mode. This may allow an attacker to run arbitrary code through the debugger.
A Flask app appears to be run in debug mode. This may allow an attacker to run arbitrary code through the debugger.
